## A Wonderful Evening

Thank you to everyone who came out to the opening night of "Earth & Fire". It was incredible to see so many familiar faces and meet new art lovers.

The dialogue between the ceramics and the wooden sculptures by James Doe created a fantastic tension in the gallery space. We are already discussing a follow-up collaboration for next year.

Notes from the kiln and upcoming projects.

## Fresh from the Kiln

We've just unloaded a new firing and the results are promising. The experiment with local clay harvested from the riverbank yielded a rich, terracotta red that works beautifully with the white slip decoration.

Join us for a weekend of wheel throwing.

## Learn the Wheel

We are excited to announce a beginner's intensive weekend workshop. Whether you are a complete novice or looking to refine your centering technique, this workshop will provide hands-on guidance.

**Topics Covered:**
1.  Wedging and clay preparation
2.  Centering on the wheel
3.  Pulling walls and shaping
4.  Trimming and finishing

Spaces are limited to 8 participants. Link in bio to register!

Acquisition of the 'Blue Series' by Modern Art London.

## The Blue Series

We are honored to have the entire "Blue Series" acquired by Modern Art London for their permanent collection. This series explored the gradients of cobalt oxide in porcelain.

The pieces will be on display in the West Wing starting next month.

The Blue Series

A curated set of large scale vessels for a private residence.

## Architectural Scale

This commission involved creating three large-scale vessels for a minimalist loft in Tribeca. The challenge was to create forms that held their own against the industrial architecture of the space.

The pieces were coiled built over several weeks and fired in a reduction atmosphere to achieve a dark, metallic finish.

Architectural Scale

A collection of responsive websites focused on clean layouts, modern aesthetics, and intuitive user experiences. Each project emphasizes clarity, accessibility, and purposeful design.

## Splitli

Splitli is a conceptual website for people seeking guidance on their fitness journey. Fitness progress is often mistaken for just getting active, but Splitli informs users that there is a better method that can
hold accountability, increase progress, and improve daily experience. The goal of the project was to create a non-intimidating experience for new gym-goers and organize helpful content in a digestible and modern format.

The website discusses what a split is, the benefits, a glossary of gym terms, and a form to match users to their best matching split. I prioritized accessibility and user experience in the production of the site, so users from all walks of life can engage in its content. The design features a clean layout, bold color palette, and structured typography to optimize user experience.

img

## Hyrax Haven

Hyrax Haven is a website for a wildlife conservation organization dedicated to raising awareness about hyraxes. These mammals are small, often overlooked, and are native to Africa and the Middle East. The goal of the project was to transform a basic informational site into a modern digital platform that encourages users to learn about the species and the conservation challenges they face. 

Through research into non-profit websites and content strategy, I developed a site structure that prioritizes education, accessibility, and user engagement. The design organizes information into focused sections, features a clean layout, nature-inspired color palette, and structured typography that presents information in a way that is approachable and visually cohesive.

img

## Bartender's Glossary

Bartender’s Glossary is a website that delivers a quick guide to create the bar’s most popular drinks and use its tools. As a bartender, I used my years of knowledge to write and design the content for the page. The goal of the project was to create a space for bartenders to educate themselves on how to use their bar to its fullest potential, make drinks that shape the customer experience, and quickly display recipes for those forgetful moments during a shift. 

The website features organized sections displaying cocktail recipes, shot recipes, and explaining bar tools. I prioritized the website to have a quick interaction time with navigation tools and a single page to decrease clicks and improve content delivery time. The website utilizes a simple color palette typography to emphasize the content and improve user experience.

A range of graphic design work including branding, print, and visual exploration. These projects highlight versatility in style, concept development, and visual communication.

## Scoop Strong

Scoop Strong is a custom recipe book I wrote and designed to help those on a fitness journey enjoy a sweet treat while also hitting their protein goals. The book is dedicated to helping users learn how to use the Ninja Creami while making high-protein ice cream recipes that support fitness goals. The goal of the project was to create a fun and practical guide that makes it easy to enjoy a treat while still focusing on balanced nutrition.

The book introduces the device, explains how it works, and walks through simple steps for creating different recipes. I wrote the content to be clear and approachable so the recipes feel easy to follow and repeat. The design organizes information into introductory sections, recipe sections, and uses a

User-centered interface and experience design exploring structure, usability, and interaction. These projects focus on creating seamless, engaging digital experiences through thoughtful design decisions.

## Everyday Energy

Everyday Energy is an app that tracks user’s health and offers an approachable app experience. I find that many health apps can be difficult to navigate and non-encompassing, so I created Everyday Energy to put all the important information in one spot. The goal of the project was to create a space for users to access their daily health statistics to aid in their fitness journey and make wellness easy.

The app features organized sections displaying statistics such as mood, sleep, steps, activity, and screen time. It also offers other services such as a mood tracker, sleep tracker, and daily journal. I prioritized the website to have a welcoming prescence and intuitive navigation through its color palette, layut, and typography.

Hand-built organic shapes inspired by coastal landscapes.

## Organic Growth

Unlike wheel-thrown pieces, these vessels were built using coil and slab techniques, allowing for asymmetry and organic imperfections. The inspiration comes from the eroded cliffs and stone formations of the coast.

Each piece is unique, with textures created by pressing local stones and shells into the soft clay before firing.

Organic Growth

Unlike wheel-thrown pieces, these vessels were built using coil and slab techniques, allowing for asymmetry and organic imperfections. The inspiration comes from the eroded cliffs and stone formations of the coast.

Each piece is unique, with textures created by pressing local stones and shells into the soft clay before firing.
A study in surface treatment and natural glazes.

## Surface and Depth

This collection focuses on the tactile experience of ceramics. Using heavy grog clay bodies and shino glazes, the surface becomes a landscape of its own.

The firing process was done in a wood kiln, introducing ash deposits that create unpredictable and beautiful variations in color and texture.

Surface and Depth

This collection focuses on the tactile experience of ceramics. Using heavy grog clay bodies and shino glazes, the surface becomes a landscape of its own.

The firing process was done in a wood kiln, introducing ash deposits that create unpredictable and beautiful variations in color and texture.
Exploring the simplicity of shape and silence in clay.

## The Essence of Reduction

In this series, I explored the concept of reduction—stripping away the unnecessary to reveal the essential form. The clay is left relatively unadorned, allowing the silhouette to speak for itself.

> "Perfection is achieved, not when there is nothing more to add, but when there is nothing left to take away." - Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

The process involved wheel throwing followed by extensive trimming to achieve thin, delicate walls that contradict the visual weight of the material.
